sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: CCPA Final Regulations: Business Compliance Guide
feed.summary: The CCPA Final Regulations introduce significant new obligations for businesses to protect consumer data, effective January 1, 2026. Key changes include mandatory annual cybersecurity audits, comprehensive risk assessments for high-risk data processing, and enhanced governance requirements for Automated Decision-Making Technology (ADMT). This guide outlines these new provisions, their applicability criteria, implementation timelines, and ongoing compliance obligations, stressing the importance of proactive preparation to avoid substantial penalties.

feed.title: NYSE Content Update: U.S.-Based Diversified Energy to Ring Bell for 25 Years Since its Founding
feed.summary: The New York Stock Exchange announced that Diversified Energy (NYSE: DEC) will celebrate its 25th anniversary of founding by ringing the closing bell. The company's CEO, Rusty Hutson, will discuss why the NYSE is their premier listing venue. The update also covers other market insights, including early Monday market slides and Robert Herjavec discussing cyber threats.

feed.title: Wells Fargo & Company Lowers S&P Global (NYSE:SPGI) Price Target to $525.00
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Company has reduced its price target for S&P Global (NYSE:SPGI) to $525.00 from $530.00, while maintaining an "overweight" rating. Despite the lowered target, analyst sentiment remains largely positive, with a consensus "Buy" rating and an average price target of $558.47 across multiple firms. S&P Global recently traded at $422.06, has a market cap of $128.1 billion, and reported quarterly EPS of $4.30, slightly missing consensus estimates.

feed.title: Regeneron Enters $2.1 Billion Radiopharma Collaboration with Telix Pharmaceuticals
feed.summary: Regeneron and Telix Pharmaceuticals have formed a collaboration valued at up to $2.1 billion to develop next-generation radiopharmaceutical therapies for precision oncology. The partnership leverages Regeneron's antibody discovery platform and Telix's radiopharmaceutical development capabilities, including isotope selection, manufacturing, and global distribution. The initial focus is on multiple solid tumor targets, with co-developed diagnostic agents for patient selection and treatment monitoring.

feed.title: Portnoy Law Firm Announces Class Action on Behalf of Boston Scientific Corporation Investors
feed.summary: The Portnoy Law Firm has announced a class action lawsuit on behalf of Boston Scientific Corporation (NYSE: BSX) investors who purchased securities between July 3, 2025, and February 3, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that Boston Scientific failed to disclose competitive pressures affecting its U.S. Electrophysiology market share, leading to a significant stock price drop after its Q4 and full-year 2025 earnings missed guidance. Investors have until May 4, 2026, to file a lead plaintiff motion.

feed.title: Labor secretary climbs Union Pacific's Big Boy as 61,000 visit
feed.summary: U.S. Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer joined Union Pacific CEO Jim Vena for a tour of Big Boy No. 4014 and the J.R. David Yard in Roseville, California, as part of the locomotive's coast-to-coast tour celebrating America's 250th birthday. The event drew an estimated 61,000 visitors and highlighted the railroad's historical significance in connecting the nation and driving economic growth. Big Boy No. 4014, the world's largest operating steam locomotive, commenced its tour from Sacramento, commemorating the first transcontinental railroad's origins.

feed.title: Stryker to Acquire Amplitude Vascular Systems
feed.summary: Stryker Corp. is acquiring Amplitude Vascular Systems Inc. (AVS), a medical technology firm specializing in a next-generation intravascular lithotripsy (IVL) platform for calcified peripheral arterial disease. This acquisition will enhance Stryker's Peripheral Vascular portfolio with revascularization technology, complementing its existing offerings and expanding its market presence in arterial disease treatment. The deal aims to provide physicians with expanded treatment options and improve care for patients with calcified peripheral arterial disease.

feed.title: STERIS plc stock (US8589121081): Is its infection control dominance strong enough to unlock steady h
feed.summary: STERIS plc (NYSE: STE), an Irish-domiciled company listed on Nasdaq, is presented as a defensive investment opportunity in the healthcare sector, focusing on infection prevention and sterilization solutions. The company's business model combines hardware sales with recurring service contracts, which account for over half of its revenue, providing stable income and strong margins. STERIS's dominance in a highly regulated market, especially in the US, positions it to benefit from increasing healthcare spending and patient safety priorities, while strategically utilizing bolt-on acquisitions and digital transformation to maintain its competitive edge.

feed.title: How Renewed Analyst Optimism And Rising Institutional Ownership At Citizens Financial Group (CFG) Has Changed Its Investment Story
feed.summary: Citizens Financial Group is experiencing renewed analyst optimism and increasing institutional ownership, with Altfest L J & Co. Inc. significantly boosting its stake in the bank. These developments precede the bank's upcoming earnings report, highlighting growing confidence among professional investors in Citizens' business strategies. Despite this, the article cautions that potential risks from commercial real estate exposure could still impact credit quality and profitability, urging investors to consider various scenarios.

feed.title: BMO Capital Markets Raises DuPont de Nemours Price Target to $60
feed.summary: BMO Capital Markets has increased its price target for DuPont de Nemours (NYSE: DD) from $25.10 to $60.00, maintaining an "outperform" rating. This new target represents a potential upside of 27.33% from the previous close, reflecting the company's strong fundamentals and growth potential. Other analysts have also raised their price targets, indicating growing confidence in DuPont's performance within the specialty materials, chemicals, and industrial sectors.

feed.title: Stable session for Energy Transfer stock as $19.05 support draws focus
feed.summary: Energy Transfer LP (ET) is trading at $19.12, showing a generally bullish trend despite a mild 0.36% decline today. The stock is holding above its key moving averages and maintaining bullish technical indicators, with strong buyer domination and low intraday volatility. Experts suggest that ET is expected to consolidate between $19.05 support and $19.60 resistance, with a high probability of an upside move if the $19.05 support holds.

feed.title: Autodesk Stock Faces Downgrade As Citi Warns Of Near Term Software Sector Pullback
feed.summary: Autodesk (ADSK) stock was downgraded by Citi analyst Tyler Radke from Buy to Neutral, with the price target cut to $246. This downgrade reflects a broader cautious outlook on the software sector rather than specific concerns about Autodesk's fundamentals, though the company faces near-term risks like a sales restructuring and a premium valuation. Despite Citi's bearish stance, the majority of analysts remain bullish on ADSK, with a consensus price target suggesting significant upside.

feed.title: Fenbo Holdings (FEBO) director Dai Lei submits initial insider Form 3
feed.summary: Fenbo Holdings (FEBO) director Dai Lei has submitted an initial Form 3, a mandatory statement of beneficial ownership for insiders, but the filing does not report any current share holdings or recent transactions. This initial filing serves to disclose Dai Lei's position as a director without indicating any specific trading activity or derivative positions. Investors can use this filing as a baseline to track future insider trading actions.

feed.title: Regeneron, Telix Enter Strategic Radiopharma Collaboration
feed.summary: Regeneron Pharmaceuticals and Telix Pharmaceuticals Ltd. have announced a strategic collaboration to jointly develop and commercialize next-generation radiopharmaceutical therapies. This partnership will combine Regeneron's biologics expertise, including bispecific antibody discovery, with Telix's radiopharmaceutical development platform and manufacturing capabilities. The collaboration aims to address solid tumor targets and develop radio-diagnostics, with Telix receiving an upfront payment of $40 million for initial programs and potential milestones and royalties.

feed.title: Dollar General to offer AI-enabled audio with targeted ads in select stores
feed.summary: Dollar General is expanding its in-store audio network by partnering with Qsic to deploy an AI-enabled audio network across approximately 6,000 stores, doubling its existing presence. This initiative, part of the DG Media Network, aims to deliver localized, data-driven audio content and targeted ads to customers and brand partners. The retailer is also leveraging other collaborations with Fetch, Kevel, Recess, and Criteo to enhance its retail media capabilities and provide advanced measurement to advertisers.

feed.title: The Infrastructure Giant: A Comprehensive 2026 Research Feature on Amazon.com (AMZN)
feed.summary: This 2026 research feature highlights Amazon's evolution from an e-commerce giant to a global infrastructure provider, emphasizing its crucial role in the AI arms race and its new venture into space-based telecommunications with Amazon Leo. The article details Amazon's diversified business model, strong financial performance, and leadership under Andy Jassy, while also addressing competitive pressures, regulatory risks like the FTC antitrust trial, and future growth opportunities.

feed.title: The Permian Juggernaut: A Comprehensive Research Feature on Diamondback Energy (FANG)
feed.summary: Diamondback Energy (FANG) has transformed into a dominant "Permian Juggernaut" after its 2024 merger with Endeavor Energy Resources, becoming a benchmark for operational excellence in the Midland Basin. The company focuses on capital discipline and aggressive shareholder returns, making it an attractive option for investors seeking exposure to North America's most prolific oil field. Diamondback's strategic acquisitions, vertical integration, and innovative technologies have fueled its impressive stock performance and strong financial profile, positioning it as a leader in the consolidating Permian Basin.

feed.title: Barclays Issues Positive Forecast for Simon Property Group (NYSE:SPG) Stock Price
feed.summary: Barclays has raised its price target for Simon Property Group (NYSE:SPG) to $201.00 from $193.00, maintaining an "equal weight" rating, suggesting a potential upside of 0.45%. Other analysts have also adjusted their targets, with the company currently holding an average "Hold" rating and a consensus price target of $200.14. Simon Property Group recently reported strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst estimates, and authorized a $2.00 billion share repurchase program, indicating management views the stock as undervalued.

feed.title: Lobbying Update: $20,000 of NORTHROP GRUMMAN lobbying was just disclosed
feed.summary: A recent Lobbying Disclosure Act filing revealed $20,000 in lobbying by NORTHROP GRUMMAN (NOC) during Q1 2026, focusing on Space, Missile Defense, and Advanced National Security Programs. The article also details recent congressional and insider trading activity for NOC, hedge fund movements, analyst ratings, and price targets.

feed.title: Targa Resources (NYSE:TRGP) Given New $285.00 Price Target at Truist Financial
feed.summary: Truist Financial has increased its price target for Targa Resources (NYSE:TRGP) to $285.00 from $279.00, maintaining a "buy" rating and implying a 16.76% upside. This comes as Targa Resources' shares opened at $244.09, with a market cap of $52.43 billion, and analysts project an 8.15 EPS for the current year. The consensus rating from analysts remains a "Moderate Buy" with an average target price of $257.14, despite recent insider stock sales totaling approximately $24.7 million.

feed.title: Barclays Lowers Gen Digital (NASDAQ:GEN) Price Target to $26.00
feed.summary: Barclays has reduced its price target for Gen Digital (NASDAQ:GEN) from $32.00 to $26.00, maintaining an "equal weight" rating, indicating a potential upside of 42.66% from its last close. Despite beating Q4 earnings estimates with $0.64 EPS and 25.8% year-over-year revenue growth, the cybersecurity stock is trading near its 12-month low and carries a high debt-to-equity ratio of 3.50. The company, which has a market cap of approximately $11 billion, has a consensus "Hold" rating from analysts with an average target price of $31.86.

feed.title: Public Storage price target lowered to $309 from $311 at Evercore ISI
feed.summary: Evercore ISI has reduced its price target for Public Storage (PSA) to $309 from $311, while maintaining an "In Line" rating on the shares. This adjustment is part of the firm's Q1 storage REIT preview ahead of the upcoming earnings season. The report highlights analyst expectations and recent financial activities related to Public Storage.

feed.title: Northrop Grumman advances Sentinel ICBM development By Investing.com
feed.summary: Northrop Grumman is making significant progress on its $95.7 billion Sentinel intercontinental ballistic missile program, with initial flight targeted for 2027 and operational capability by the early 2030s. The company has completed key testing milestones, assembled the first booster, and invested heavily in infrastructure and R&D for the program, which will replace the Minuteman III. Northrop Grumman's stock has risen 18.5% year-to-date, reflecting investor confidence, and analysts have recently raised price targets for the defense contractor.

feed.title: DOD formalizes $4.7 billion Lockheed Martin PAC-3 missile contract
feed.summary: The Department of Defense has awarded Lockheed Martin Corp. a $4.7 billion contract for PAC-3 Missile Segment Enhancement interceptors, with work extending until June 30, 2030, and including tasks in Huntsville. This contract supports accelerated production to increase inventory and strengthen readiness, complementing a previous agreement with Boeing to boost PAC-3 missile seeker production. These investments by the U.S. Army and through foreign military sales aim to enhance national security, industrial growth, and technological advancement.

feed.title: JPMorgan Chase & Co. Boosts Occidental Petroleum (NYSE:OXY) Price Target to $64.00
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. has raised its price target for Occidental Petroleum (NYSE:OXY) to $64.00 from $63.00, maintaining a "neutral" rating and implying a 9.35% upside. Analyst sentiment for OXY is mixed, with a consensus "Hold" rating and an average price target of $60.39. Despite a revenue miss, Occidental Petroleum recently beat EPS expectations in its latest quarterly earnings report.

feed.title: ASML (NASDAQ:ASML) Earns Buy Rating from JPMorgan Chase & Co.
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. has reiterated a "Buy" rating on ASML's stock, with other analysts generally maintaining positive outlooks and an average target price of $1,482.50. The company, a leader in photolithography systems for semiconductor manufacturing, has a market capitalization of $576.1 billion and a current share price of $1,464.87. Institutional investors and hedge funds hold a significant portion of ASML's stock.

feed.title: CF Industries (NYSE: CF): From Fertilizer Giant to Low-Carbon Energy Leader
feed.summary: CF Industries (NYSE: CF) has transformed from an agricultural fertilizer company into a leader in the low-carbon energy economy, becoming the world's largest producer of ammonia. The company is leveraging its manufacturing footprint and strategic location to capitalize on decarbonization efforts, focusing on blue ammonia and carbon capture projects. This shift, coupled with strong financial performance and a strategic leadership transition, positions CF as a significant player in both food security and clean energy, despite potential risks from feedstock volatility and policy changes.

feed.title: Fund Update: 183,643 QUANTA SERVICES (PWR) shares added to Sarasin & Partners LLP portfolio
feed.summary: Sarasin & Partners LLP has increased its holdings in Quanta Services (PWR) by adding 183,643 shares to its portfolio, as per a recent SEC 13F filing for the 03-31-2026 report period. The article also details significant institutional investor activity, insider trading, congressional stock trading, and current analyst ratings with price targets for PWR.

feed.title: Micron Stock Could Gain Another 40%, Analyst Says. Watch This Catalyst.
feed.summary: KeyBanc analysts predict that Micron Technology stock could see another 40% increase. The memory-chip maker's shares have already surged 47% this year, with upcoming earnings potentially driving further growth. Investors should monitor the company's earnings report as a key catalyst.

feed.title: AbbVie inks $745M deal with Chinese biotech Haisco for two acute pain assets
feed.summary: AbbVie has entered a $745 million deal with Chinese biotech Haisco Pharmaceutical Group for exclusive ex-China rights to two non-opioid acute pain treatment candidates, HSK55718 and HSK51155. This agreement includes a $30 million upfront payment, near-term milestones, and additional milestone payments, reflecting AbbVie's strategic interest in the NaV1.8 inhibitor market, where Vertex Pharmaceuticals has already seen success. The collaboration also aligns with Haisco's goal of international expansion and innovation-driven growth in the pharmaceutical industry.

feed.title: Conagra Brands names Smucker executive John Brase as new CEO
feed.summary: Conagra Brands has appointed J.M. Smucker executive John Brase as its new CEO, effective June 1. Brase will succeed Sean Connolly, who is stepping down after 11 years, as Conagra navigates softening consumer spending and global trade tensions. The company recently lowered its annual profit forecast and is addressing shifting consumer tastes, including those influenced by GLP-1 drugs for weight loss.

feed.title: Deere reaches 'right to repair' settlement
feed.summary: Deere & Company has settled a multidistrict "right to repair" lawsuit for $99 million, resolving allegations of monopolizing repair services. The settlement, which includes no finding of wrongdoing, will fund a class settlement and ensure Deere continues to provide customers with access to repair resources like tools and manuals. This move follows Deere's efforts to expand self-repair resources and an FTC lawsuit filed in early 2025.

feed.title: Dutch regulator notifies European Commission of plan to seek EU approval for Tesla's FSD
feed.summary: The Dutch vehicle authority RDW has informed the European Commission of its intention to seek EU-wide approval for Tesla's Full-Self Driving (FSD) system. This move was confirmed by Bernd van Nieuwenhoven, general manager of type approvals at the regulator, to Reuters. The notification suggests a step towards broader adoption of Tesla's autonomous driving technology across the European Union.

feed.title: Seaport Global Sticks to Its Buy Rating for Constellation Energy Corporation (CEG)
feed.summary: Seaport Global has reiterated its Buy rating for Constellation Energy Corporation (CEG), setting a price target of $330.00. This follows similar Buy ratings from other analysts like Barclays, while Mizuho Securities maintained a Hold rating. Constellation Energy reported a quarterly revenue of $5.46 billion and a net profit of $432 million for the quarter ending December 31.

feed.title: Conagra Brands names JM Smucker executive as new CEO
feed.summary: Conagra Brands has appointed John Brase, formerly of J.M. Smucker, as its new President and CEO, effective June 1. Brase will replace Sean Connolly, who has led the company since 2015. This leadership change comes as Conagra faces slumping sales due to shifts in consumer spending and preferences, with Brase expected to leverage his extensive experience in consumer packaged goods to drive growth and profitability.

feed.title: Japan Post unit trims AFLAC (NYSE: AFL) stake with 86K-share sale
feed.summary: Japan Post Holdings Co., Ltd., a 10% owner of Aflac Inc., indirectly sold 86,155 shares of Aflac common stock in open-market transactions on April 9, 2026. The sales occurred at weighted average prices ranging from $112.50 to $113.74 per share. Following these transactions, Japan Post-associated entities indirectly held 51,636,945 Aflac shares.

feed.title: Fidelity National Information Services (NYSE:FIS) Price Target Cut to $65.00 by Analysts at The Goldman Sachs Group
feed.summary: The Goldman Sachs Group has cut its price target for Fidelity National Information Services (FIS) to $65.00 from $70.00 but maintained a "buy" rating, implying a potential 48.9% upside. This adjustment comes amidst similar target cuts from other brokers, yet the analyst consensus remains a "Moderate Buy" with an average target of $68.71. FIS recently reported slightly missed EPS at $1.68 but beat revenue estimates at $2.81 billion, and its CEO made a significant insider purchase of approximately $1.0 million.

feed.title: Equity Residential (NYSE:EQR) Given New $67.00 Price Target at Royal Bank Of Canada
feed.summary: Royal Bank of Canada has adjusted its price target for Equity Residential (NYSE:EQR) to $67.00 from $68.00 while maintaining an "outperform" rating, indicating a potential 10.43% upside. This adjustment follows the company missing quarterly EPS and revenue expectations, though Equity Residential has issued Q1 and FY 2026 guidance. Despite the target cut, analysts surveyed by MarketBeat hold a consensus "Hold" rating with an average target of $69.53.

feed.title: Devon Energy (NYSE:DVN) Price Target Raised to $53.00
feed.summary: Jefferies Financial Group has raised its price target for Devon Energy (NYSE:DVN) to $53.00 from $44.00, maintaining a "hold" rating and implying a 9.35% upside. This adjustment aligns with a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating and an average price target of $53.11 from other firms like Truist, BMO, RBC, and Morgan Stanley, which have also increased their targets for the energy company. Devon Energy recently reported a slight earnings beat with $0.82 EPS against estimates of $0.81, and revenues of $4.12 billion, continuing to see institutional investor activity.

feed.title: DuPont de Nemours (NYSE:DD) Price Target Raised to $60.00 at BMO Capital Markets
feed.summary: BMO Capital Markets raised its price target for DuPont de Nemours (NYSE:DD) to $60.00 from $25.10, maintaining an "outperform" rating, indicating a potential upside of 27.33%. Several other analysts also increased their price targets on DD recently. The company recently reported better-than-expected quarterly EPS of $0.46 and provided FY2026 guidance of $2.25–$2.30 EPS.

feed.title: FB Financial earnings on deck: Can loan growth offset margin pressure?
feed.summary: FB Financial Corp is expected to report its first-quarter results, with analysts looking for strong loan growth and net interest margin performance to offset potential revenue and earnings declines. The bank has strong analyst support and a compelling valuation, despite anticipated sequential pullbacks from its strong fourth-quarter performance. Investors will be watching whether the bank's market positioning and cost savings from its SSBK acquisition can maintain profitability amidst industry-wide revenue pressures.

feed.title: Equinor (NYSE: EQNR) moves to cancel 166M shares in capital cut
feed.summary: Equinor ASA's board of directors has proposed a significant capital reduction, aiming to decrease the company's share capital by NOK 415,146,180.00. This involves canceling and redeeming a total of 166,058,472 shares, following previous share repurchases authorized in May 2025. The proposal, which also includes the redemption of shares held by the Norwegian State, will be subject to approval at an upcoming general meeting.

feed.title: Humana (NYSE:HUM) Price Target Raised to $220.00
feed.summary: Truist Financial has increased its price target for Humana (NYSE:HUM) to $220.00 from $200.00, reiterating a "hold" rating on the insurance provider's stock. This new target implies a potential upside of approximately 15.59% from Humana's current price. The company recently reported quarterly earnings that surpassed expectations, with an EPS of ($3.96) against an estimated ($4.01) and revenue of $32.64 billion.

feed.title: Visa Inc. (NYSE: V) launches exchange offer for Class B shares
feed.summary: Visa Inc. has initiated an exchange offer for eligible Class B common stock, allowing holders to swap them for new Class B-3 and Class C common stock, and convertible Class A common stock. Participation requires the execution of a Letter of Transmittal and a Makewhole Agreement, which obligates holders to reimburse Visa for certain future U.S. covered litigation. Shares acquired through the offer will be canceled.

feed.title: T. Rowe Price Reports $1.71 Trillion in March Assets
feed.summary: T. Rowe Price Group, Inc. reported $1.71 trillion in assets under management as of March 31, 2026, marking a decrease from $1.803 trillion in February due to $3.2 billion in net outflows for the month. The largest outflows were seen in its equity and multi-asset portfolios. The company is set to release its Q1 2026 earnings and host an earnings call on April 30, 2026.

feed.title: The Science of Scale: An In-Depth Look at Regeneron Pharmaceuticals (REGN) in 2026
feed.summary: Regeneron Pharmaceuticals (REGN) is a biotechnology titan known for its R&D focus, navigating challenges like patent cliffs by transitioning its Eylea franchise and expanding Dupixent into new markets. The company maintains elite profitability and a strong balance sheet, with high institutional ownership and a "Moderate Buy" analyst consensus. Its future growth hinges on new oncology pipelines, genetic therapies, and successful market entries in obesity and other rare diseases.

feed.title: BMO raises Duke Energy stock price target to $143 on growth upside
feed.summary: BMO Capital has raised its price target for Duke Energy (NYSE:DUK) to $143 from $136, maintaining an Outperform rating, citing potential for earnings and growth upside, especially from data center onboarding activity. The firm anticipates regulatory updates and continued large load activity to be key investor focus during the upcoming first-quarter earnings report on May 5. Despite the stock trading near its 52-week high, InvestingPro data suggests it might be overvalued at current levels.

feed.title: Citigroup Inc.'s Long Position In H-Shares Of Ganfeng Lithium Increases To 5.7% On Apr 2 From 5.08%, HKEX Filing Shows
feed.summary: Citigroup Inc. has increased its long position in the H-shares of Ganfeng Lithium to 5.7% as of April 2, up from 5.08%. This change was disclosed in a recent filing with the Hong Kong Stock Exchange (HKEX). The report indicates a notable shift in Citigroup's investment in the lithium producer.

feed.title: Day One Biopharmaceuticals stock reaches 52-week high of $21.48
feed.summary: Day One Biopharmaceuticals (DAWN) stock recently hit a 52-week high of $21.48, showcasing a 201.54% impressive 1-year return and a 130% year-to-date gain, with a market capitalization of $2.22 billion. This surge is likely due to biopharmaceutical advancements and strategic initiatives, despite TradingPro suggesting it's currently overvalued. However, the company is being acquired by Servier for $2.5 billion ($21.50 per share), leading to several analyst downgrades to Neutral or Hold ratings.

feed.title: SEC Dismisses FAT Brands Enforcement Case
feed.summary: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission has dismissed its civil enforcement action against FAT Brands, Inc. and several of its executives, including Andrew Wiederhorn, Ron Roe, and Rebecca D. Hershinger. The dismissal, filed on March 27, 2026, was made with prejudice, meaning the claims cannot be refiled, and was based on evidence developed during discovery. This resolution concludes a high-profile securities case without the imposition of monetary penalties or injunctive relief against the defendants.
